channel.title: Recipes on tjappen.
channel.description: Recent content in Recipes on tjappen.

item.title: Duckbreast á l'orange
item.description:
Mix orange zest, juice, honey, soy sauce, and pepper in a zip-lock bag for the marinade.

To prep the duck breast: score the duck fat layer in a diamond pattern. Add to the marinade for 1-24 hours.

Cooking the duck breast: remove duck from marinade. Place duck fat-side down in a cold frying pan.

Slowly render the fat over medium heat for 12-15 minutes until it is golden brown and crisp.

item.title: Italian Sausage Gnocchi
item.description:
Slice the garlic into thin slices (1mm thick).

Bring a pan of water to a boil for the gnocchi.

Heat a frying pan over medium-high heat with olive oil.

Cut open sausages, crumble the meat into the pan in bite-sized chunks.

Brown the sausage chunks on both sides, then lower the heat.

Add garlic slices and chili flakes; sweat for 1-2 minutes.

Salt the boiling water and cook gnocchi (3-4 minutes).

item.title: Jackie's Spinach Casserole
item.description:
Preheat the oven to 200C (conventional oven).

Thaw the frozen spinach in the microwave.

Cook the (vegan) minced meat in a pan until brown and crumbly. Meanwhile, boil the 4 eggs for about 10 minutes until hard-boiled.

In an oven dish, mix the thawed spinach, the cooked minced meat, and the grated cheese. Peel and slice the boiled eggs, and add them to the dish. Cover the top of the mixture with the slices of puff pastry.

item.title: Korean BBQ chicken wraps
item.description:
Mix spices, Gochujang, honey, and soy sauce. Massage the marinade into the chicken. Use two thirds of the garlic powder, sesame seeds and honey.

Bake the chicken at 200C for 25-30 minutes.

For the salad: thinly slice the cucumbers and onions, then mix with grated carrot, rice vinegar, sriracha, and remaining sesame seeds.

For the slightly spicy sauce: mix yogurt, mayo, gochujang, remaining honey, and remaining garlic powder. In case the consistency is too thick, add a splash of milk.

item.title: Modern Pasta Carbonara
item.description:
Dice the asparagus into tiny pieces (0.5cm each). Do not use the tips, save thosse for a different recipe. Cut the guanciale into bite sized cubes.

In a bowl, mix the egg and egg yolks, and slowly add the grated parmesan until it forms a semi-liquid paste.

Boil salted water and cook the pasta al dente.

Fry the guanciale over medium-high heat in a dry pan until the fat renders out, then add the asparagus and lots of pepper. Fry until the asparagus is cooked and guanciale is crispy.

item.title: Pasta Alla Norma
item.description:
Preheat the oven to 220C fan.

Using a peeler and working from top to bottom of each aubergine, shave off long alternating strips of peel so that they look striped, like zebras. Cut widthways into 1cm thick slices and place in a bowl with 75ml of oil, 3/4 teaspoon of salt and a generous grind of pepper. Mix well, then spread out on two large baking paper-lined trays. Roast for 30-35 minutes, until dark golden-brown. Remove from the oven and set aside to cool.

item.title: Pasta with salmon and spinach
item.description:
Cut the salmon into bite-sized pieces.

Bring salted water to a boil and cook the pasta until slightly under al dente.

Heat olive oil in a large saucepan or wok over medium-high heat.

Bake the salmon for 1-2 minutes until slightly golden.

Add the spinach in batches, allowing the excess water to evaporate before adding more.

Drain the pasta, reserving 1 glass of pasta water.

Toast pine nuts in a separate dry pan for 1-2 minutes until golden.

item.title: Ragù Bolognese
item.description:
Finely dice the onion, cut the celery and carrot in to small cubes, roughly 2-3mm each.

Preheat your oven to 140 degrees celsius.

Add some olive oil in to a large pan (that can be placed in the oven, preferably a cast-iron dutch oven) and add your onions, celery and carrot.

Fruit the vegetables on low to medium low temp, until soft and sweet.

Remove vegetables from the pan, and wipe the pan clean with a paper towel.

item.title: Sweet potato salad
item.description:
Preheat the oven to 200C.

Dice sweet potato and carrot into half-circles (approx. 0.5cm thick) and place on a baking tray.

Add olive oil, salt, and pepper; bake for 20-25 minutes.

Add asparagus tips and bake for another 10 minutes.

Finely dice the red onion, extract lemon juice, and crumble the feta.

In a bowl, mix the roasted vegetables, lamb’s lettuce, onion, and feta.

Whisk lemon juice with 2 tablespoons of olive oil.

item.title: Vegan Massaman Curry
item.description:
Heat 1/2 cup (about 120ml) of the coconut milk in a large pot or wok over medium heat until it begins to bubble and the oil starts to slightly separate. Add the red curry paste, cumin seeds, coriander seeds, cinnamon, and nutmeg. Stir well and fry for 1-2 minutes until deeply fragrant.

Pour in the remaining coconut milk along with the maple syrup, soy sauce, peanut butter, lime juice, and tamarind paste. Stir continuously until the peanut butter completely dissolves and the curry base is smooth.
